title: Keep all UI updates on the main thread
description: Mutate UIKit views and SwiftUI observable state only on the main thread, hopping back from any background work.
body: Use when updating UI after async work (network, disk, computation). Do not touch UIKit views or published state from a background queue, and do not assume a completion handler runs on main. Actions: perform heavy work off the main thread, then hop to the main actor / main queue (DispatchQueue.main.async or @MainActor) to apply UI changes; mark UI-driving types @MainActor. Validation signals include the Main Thread Checker showing no violations and no intermittent UI corruption. Risk boundary: updating UIKit off the main thread causes nondeterministic crashes and corruption that surface only under timing — a background UI mutation is a real defect even when it 'usually works'.
